revision.text:
{{日本の寺院|名称=|画像=|画像説明=|所在地=|位置=大阪府堺市堺区浜寺石津町東3-11-5|緯度度=|緯度分=|緯度秒=|経度度=|経度分=|経度秒=|山号=乳岡山|院号=|宗旨=浄土宗|宗派=|寺格=|本尊=[[阿弥陀如来]]|創建年=|開山=円誉養雲比丘尼|開基=|中興年=|中興=|正式名=|別称=|札所等=[[和泉西国三十三箇所]]第11番|文化財=|公式HP=http://sasaki3.net/old_contents/nenbutuji/index1.html|公式HP名=堺市 浄土宗 念佛寺|地図=|地図2=}} '''念仏寺'''（ねんぶつじ）は、大阪府[[堺市]]にある[[浄土宗]]の寺院。 ==概要== [[貞享]]年間（1684年-1688年）の開山とされるが、史実の開山位牌に「円誉養雲比丘尼」「[[享保]]三年戊戌六月六日」と記されている[http://sasaki3.net/old_contents/nenbutuji/index3.html 乳岡山 念佛寺 縁起]。 昭和55年（1980年）に、現在地に移転。 == 御詠歌 == ねんぶつの こえにひかれて ちのおかへ まいるこころは やみもはれゆく == 交通 == *南海バス「霞ヶ丘」から徒歩10分 *阪和線[[上野芝駅]]から徒歩25分 == 脚注 ==
== 外部リンク == *[http://www12.plala.or.jp/HOUJI//otera-2/newpage568.htm 念仏寺（堺市）] - 大阪再発見！ {{Buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ねんふつし}} [[Category:浄土宗の寺院]] [[Category:堺市の寺]] [[Category:堺区の歴史]]

{{日本の寺院 |名称=念佛寺 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=堺市}} |画像説明= |所在地=大阪府堺市西区浜寺石津町東3-11-5 |位置= |緯度度=|緯度分=|緯度秒= |経度度=|経度分=|経度秒= |山号=乳岡山 |院号= |宗旨= |宗派=[[浄土宗]] |寺格= |本尊=[[阿弥陀如来]] |創建年= |開山=養雲尼 |開基= |中興年= |中興= |正式名= |別称= |札所等=[[和泉西国三十三箇所]]第11番 |文化財= |公式HP=http://sasaki3.net/old_contents/nenbutuji/index1.html |公式HP名=堺市 浄土宗 念佛寺 |地図= |地図2= }}
'''念仏寺'''（ねんぶつじ）は、[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[西区 (堺市)|西区]]にある[[浄土宗]]の寺院。
==概要== 山号の乳丘山は[[百舌鳥古墳群]]の最西端にあり、[[石津太神社]]の神宮寺とされ、「お山の寺」と呼ばれたという。昔は村内に伽藍があり、付近の[[上石津町|上石津]]を観音寺町ともいった。
史実では開山位牌に「円誉養雲比丘尼 享保三年戊戌六月六日」と記されてあり<ref>[http://sasaki3.net/old_contents/nenbutuji/index3.html 乳岡山 念佛寺 縁起]</ref>、[[貞享]]年間（[[1684年]]-[[1688年]]）に養雲尼によって再建されたことは確かである。
昭和55年（1980年）、土地開発の煽りを受けて現在地に移転。
== 交通 == *南海バス「霞ヶ丘」から徒歩10分 *阪和線[[上野芝駅]]から徒歩25分
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *『和泉西国三十三所めぐり ふる里の観音さま』和泉西国会
{{Buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ねんふつし}} [[Category:浄土宗の寺院]] [[Category:堺市の寺]] [[Category:堺区の歴史]]

{{日本の寺院 |名称=念佛寺 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=堺市}} |画像説明= |所在地=大阪府堺市西区浜寺石津町東3-11-5 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|21.5|N|135|27|35.5|E}} |山号=乳岡山 |院号= |宗旨= |宗派=[[浄土宗]] |寺格= |本尊=[[阿弥陀如来]] |創建年= |開山=養雲尼 |開基= |中興年= |中興= |正式名= |別称= |札所等=[[和泉西国三十三箇所]]第11番 |文化財= |公式HP=http://sasaki3.net/old_contents/nenbutuji/index1.html |公式HP名=堺市 浄土宗 念佛寺 |地図= |地図2= }}

revision.text:
{{infobox religious building | name = {{ill|Nenbutsu-ji|ja|念佛寺}} | image = [[File:Nenbutsuji Temple Sakai City.jpg|thumb]]
Haiden | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Jōdo Sect|ja|浄土宗}} | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Sakai City|ja|堺市}} {{ill|Nishi Ward (Sakai City)|ja|西区 (堺市)}} {{ill|Hamadera Ishizutsumachi Higashi|ja|浜寺石津町東}} 3-11-5 | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|21.5|N|135|27|35.5|E|display=inline,title}} | 山号 = {{ill|Chikuaoyama|ja|乳岡山}} | 宗派 = {{ill|Jōdo Sect|ja|浄土宗}} | 本尊 = {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}} | 開山 = {{ill|Yōun-ni|ja|養雲尼}} | 札所等 = {{ill|Izumi Saigoku 33 Kasho|ja|和泉西国三十三箇所}} 第11番 | 公式HP = http://sasaki3.net/old_contents/nenbutuji/index1.html | 公式HP名 = Sakai City Jōdo Sect Nenbutsu-ji }} '''Nenbutsu-ji''' (念佛寺) is a Buddhist temple of the Jōdo sect located in Nishi Ward, {{ill|Sakai City|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. == Overview == The temple’s mountain name, Chikuaoyama (乳岡山), is situated at the westernmost end of the {{ill|Hyakusetori Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} and is associated with the shrine-temple of {{ill|Ishizutsuta Shrine|ja|石津太神社}}, hence once being called "The Temple of the Mountain." In ancient times, a full temple complex (伽藍) existed within the village, and the nearby area of {{ill|Kami Ishizutsu-machi|ja|上石津}} was also known as Kannonji-machi. Historically, the founder’s memorial tablet recorded the inscription "En'yo Yōun Hikyuni, 6th day of June, in the 3rd year of {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}}" indicating that the temple was indeed rebuilt by Yōun-ni during the {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} period. In Showa 55 (1980), due to land development pressures, the temple was relocated to its present site. == Access == * A 10‑minute walk from the {{ill|Nankai Bus|ja|南海バス}} stop "Kasumigaoka." * A 25‑minute walk from {{ill|Ueno-shiba Station|ja|上野芝駅}} on the {{ill|Hankawa Line|ja|阪和線}}. == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == References == * 『{{ill|Izumi Saigoku 33 Kasho Meguri Furusato no Kannon-sama|ja|和泉西国三十三所めぐり ふる里の観音さま}}』, Izumi Saigoku-kai. {{Buddhism-stub}} [[Category:Buddhist Temples of the Jōdo Sect]] [[Category:Temples in Sakai City]] [[Category:History of Nishi Ward, Sakai City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishi Ward, Sakai City]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nenbutsu-ji (Sakai)]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nenbutsu-ji (Sakai)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nenbutsu-ji (Sakai)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nenbutsu-ji (Sakai)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nenbutsu-ji (Sakai)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nenbutsu-ji (Sakai)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:%E5%BF%B5%E4%BB%8F%E5%AF%BA (%E5%A0%BA%E5%B8%82)]][[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:念佛寺]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nenbutsu-ji (Sakai)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nenbutsu-ji (Sakai)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nenbutsu-ji (Sakai)}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:500 shortest pages as of 03-29-2025]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nenbutsu-ji (Sakai)]][[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]][[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

In ancient times, a full temple complex (伽藍) existed within the village, and the nearby area of {{ill|Kami Ishizutsu-machi|ja|上石津}} was also known as Kannonji-machi. Historically, the founder’s memorial tablet recorded the inscription "En'yo Yōun Hikyuni, 6th day of June, in the 3rd year of {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}}" indicating that the temple was indeed rebuilt by Yōun-ni during the {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} period. revision.text:
{{日本の寺院 |名称=念佛寺 |画像=[[File:Nenbutsuji Temple Sakai City.jpg|250px]] |画像説明= |所在地=大阪府堺市西区浜寺石津町東3-11-5 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|21.5|N|135|27|35.5|E}} |山号=乳岡山 |院号= |宗旨= |宗派=[[浄土宗]] |寺格= |本尊=[[阿弥陀如来]] |創建年= |開山=養雲尼 |開基= |中興年= |中興= |正式名= |別称= |札所等=[[和泉西国三十三箇所]]第11番 |文化財= |公式HP=http://sasaki3.net/old_contents/nenbutuji/index1.html |公式HP名=堺市 浄土宗 念佛寺 |地図= |地図2= }}
'''念仏寺'''（ねんぶつじ）は、[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[西区 (堺市)|西区]]にある[[浄土宗]]の寺院。
==概要== 山号の乳丘山は[[百舌鳥古墳群]]の最西端にあり、[[石津太神社]]の神宮寺とされ、「お山の寺」と呼ばれたという。昔は村内に伽藍があり、付近の[[上石津町|上石津]]を観音寺町ともいった。
史実では開山位牌に「円誉養雲比丘尼 享保三年戊戌六月六日」と記されてあり<ref>[http://sasaki3.net/old_contents/nenbutuji/index3.html 乳岡山 念佛寺 縁起]</ref>、[[貞享]]年間（[[1684年]]-[[1688年]]）に養雲尼によって再建されたことは確かである。
昭和55年（1980年）、土地開発の煽りを受けて現在地に移転。
== 交通 == *南海バス「霞ヶ丘」から徒歩10分 *阪和線[[上野芝駅]]から徒歩25分
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *『和泉西国三十三所めぐり ふる里の観音さま』和泉西国会
{{Buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ねんふつし さかいし}} [[Category:浄土宗の寺院]] [[Category:堺市の寺]] [[Category:堺市西区の歴史]] [[Category:堺市西区の建築物]]

Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Jōdo-shū|Jōdo Sect]] | location = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] [[:en:Sakai|Sakai City]] [[:en:Nishi-ku, Sakai|Nishi Ward (Sakai City)]] {{ill|Hamadera Ishizutsumachi Higashi|ja|浜寺石津町東|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamadera Ishizutsumachi Higashi|qq=}} 3-11-5 | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|21.5|N|135|27|35.5|E|display=inline,title}} | 山号 = {{ill|Chikuaoyama|ja|乳岡山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuaoyama|qq=}} | 宗派 = [[:en:Jōdo-shū|Jōdo Sect]] | 本尊 = [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] | 開山 = {{ill|Yōun-ni|ja|養雲尼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōun-ni|qq=}} | 札所等 = {{ill|Izumi Saigoku 33 Kasho|ja|和泉西国三十三箇所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Saigoku 33 Kasho|qq=}} 第11番 | 公式HP = http://sasaki3.net/old_contents/nenbutuji/index1.html | 公式HP名 = Sakai City Jōdo Sect Nenbutsu-ji }} '''Nenbutsu-ji''' (念佛寺) is a Buddhist temple of the Jōdo sect located in Nishi Ward, [[:en:Sakai|Sakai City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. == Overview == The temple’s mountain name, Chikuaoyama (乳岡山), is situated at the westernmost end of the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]] and is associated with the shrine-temple of {{ill|Ishizutsuta Shrine|ja|石津太神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizutsuta Shrine|qq=}}, hence once being called "The Temple of the Mountain." In ancient times, a full temple complex (伽藍) existed within the village, and the nearby area of {{ill|Kami Ishizutsu-machi|ja|上石津|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Ishizutsu-machi|qq=}} was also known as Kannonji-machi. Historically, the founder’s memorial tablet recorded the inscription "En'yo Yōun Hikyuni, 6th day of June, in the 3rd year of [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]]" indicating that the temple was indeed rebuilt by Yōun-ni during the [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] period. In Showa 55 (1980), due to land development pressures, the temple was relocated to its present site. == Access == * A 10‑minute walk from the {{ill|Nankai Bus|ja|南海バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nankai Bus|qq=}} stop "Kasumigaoka." * A 25‑minute walk from [[:en:Uenoshiba Station|Ueno-shiba Station]] on the [[:en:Hanwa Line|Hankawa Line]]. == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == References == * 『{{ill|Izumi Saigoku 33 Kasho Meguri Furusato no Kannon-sama|ja|和泉西国三十三所めぐり ふる里の観音さま|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Saigoku 33 Kasho Meguri Furusato no Kannon-sama|qq=}}』, Izumi Saigoku-kai. {{Buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|念仏寺 (堺市)|version=89988578|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buddhist Temples of the Jōdo Sect]] [[Category:Temples in Sakai City]] [[Category:History of Nishi Ward, Sakai City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishi Ward, Sakai City]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Nenbutsu-ji (Sakai)]] [[ja:%E5%BF%B5%E4%BB%8F%E5%AF%BA (%E5%A0%BA%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:念佛寺]] [[Category:Temples in Sakai, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:堺市西区の歴史]] [[Category:Jōdo-shū temples]] [[ja:念仏寺 (堺市)]]

In Showa 55 (1980), due to land development pressures, the temple was relocated to its present site. == Access == * A 10‑minute walk from the {{ill|Nankai Bus|ja|南海バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nankai Bus|qq=}} stop "Kasumigaoka." * A 25‑minute walk from [[:en:Uenoshiba Station|Ueno-shiba Station]] on the [[:en:Hanwa Line|Hankawa Line]]. == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == References == * 『{{ill|Izumi Saigoku 33 Kasho Meguri Furusato no Kannon-sama|ja|和泉西国三十三所めぐり ふる里の観音さま|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Saigoku 33 Kasho Meguri Furusato no Kannon-sama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』, Izumi Saigoku-kai. {{Buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|念仏寺 (堺市)|version=89988578|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Sakai City]] [[Category:History of Nishi Ward, Sakai City]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Nenbutsu-ji (Sakai)]] [[ja:%E5%BF%B5%E4%BB%8F%E5%AF%BA (%E5%A0%BA%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:念佛寺]] [[Category:Temples in Sakai, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Jōdo-shū temples]] [[ja:念仏寺 (堺市)]]

Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Jōdo-shū|Jōdo Sect]] | location = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] [[:en:Sakai|Sakai City]] [[:en:Nishi-ku, Sakai|Nishi Ward (Sakai City)]] {{ill|Hamadera Ishizutsumachi Higashi|ja|浜寺石津町東|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamadera Ishizutsumachi Higashi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3-11-5 | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|21.5|N|135|27|35.5|E|display=inline,title}} | 山号 = {{ill|Chikuaoyama|ja|乳岡山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuaoyama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 宗派 = [[:en:Jōdo-shū|Jōdo Sect]] | 本尊 = [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] | 開山 = {{ill|Yōun-ni|ja|養雲尼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōun-ni|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 札所等 = {{ill|Izumi Saigoku 33 Kasho|ja|和泉西国三十三箇所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Saigoku 33 Kasho|qq=}} 第11番 | 公式HP = http://sasaki3.net/old_contents/nenbutuji/index1.html | 公式HP名 = Sakai City Jōdo Sect Nenbutsu-ji }} '''Nenbutsu-ji''' (念佛寺) is a Buddhist temple of the Jōdo sect located in Nishi Ward, [[:en:Sakai|Sakai City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. == Overview == The temple’s mountain name, Chikuaoyama (乳岡山), is situated at the westernmost end of the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]] and is associated with the shrine-temple of {{ill|Ishizutsuta Shrine|ja|石津太神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizutsuta Shrine|qq=}}, hence once being called "The Temple of the Mountain." In ancient times, a full temple complex (伽藍) existed within the village, and the nearby area of {{ill|Kami Ishizutsu-machi|ja|上石津|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Ishizutsu-machi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was also known as Kannonji-machi. Historically, the founder’s memorial tablet recorded the inscription "En'yo Yōun Hikyuni, 6th day of June, in the 3rd year of [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]]" indicating that the temple was indeed rebuilt by Yōun-ni during the [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] period. In Showa 55 (1980), due to land development pressures, the temple was relocated to its present site. == Access == * A 10‑minute walk from the {{ill|Nankai Bus|ja|南海バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nankai Bus|qq=}} stop "Kasumigaoka." * A 25‑minute walk from [[:en:Uenoshiba Station|Ueno-shiba Station]] on the [[:en:Hanwa Line|Hankawa Line]]. == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == References == * 『{{ill|Izumi Saigoku 33 Kasho Meguri Furusato no Kannon-sama|ja|和泉西国三十三所めぐり ふる里の観音さま|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Saigoku 33 Kasho Meguri Furusato no Kannon-sama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』, Izumi Saigoku-kai. {{Buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|念仏寺 (堺市)|version=89988578|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Nenbutsu-ji (Sakai)]] [[sn:E5%BF%B5%E4%BB%8F%E5%AF%BA (%E5%A0%BA%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Redo these jawiki links]] [[ja:念佛寺]] [[Category:Temples in Sakai, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Jōdo-shū temples]] [[ja:念仏寺 (堺市)]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]]

In ancient times, a full temple complex (伽藍) existed within the village, and the nearby area of {{ill|Kami Ishizutsu-machi|ja|上石津|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Ishizutsu-machi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was also known as Kannonji-machi. In Showa 55 (1980), due to land development pressures, the temple was relocated to its present site. == Access == * A 10‑minute walk from the {{ill|Nankai Bus|ja|南海バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nankai Bus|qq=}} stop "Kasumigaoka." * A 25‑minute walk from [[:en:Uenoshiba Station|Ueno-shiba Station]] on the [[:en:Hanwa Line|Hankawa Line]]. == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == References == * 『{{ill|Izumi Saigoku 33 Kasho Meguri Furusato no Kannon-sama|ja|和泉西国三十三所めぐり ふる里の観音さま|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Saigoku 33 Kasho Meguri Furusato no Kannon-sama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』, Izumi Saigoku-kai. {{Buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|念仏寺 (堺市)|version=89988578|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Nenbutsu-ji (Sakai)]] [[sn:E5%BF%B5%E4%BB%8F%E5%AF%BA (%E5%A0%BA%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:念佛寺]] [[Category:Temples in Sakai, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Jōdo-shū temples]] [[ja:念仏寺 (堺市)]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]]

Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Jōdo-shū|Jōdo Sect]] | location = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] [[:en:Sakai|Sakai City]] [[:en:Nishi-ku, Sakai|Nishi Ward (Sakai City)]] {{ill|Hamadera Ishizutsumachi Higashi|ja|浜寺石津町東|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamadera Ishizutsumachi Higashi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3-11-5 | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|21.5|N|135|27|35.5|E|display=inline,title}} | 山号 = {{ill|Chikuaoyama|ja|乳岡山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuaoyama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 宗派 = [[:en:Jōdo-shū|Jōdo Sect]] | 本尊 = [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] | 開山 = {{ill|Yōun-ni|ja|養雲尼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōun-ni|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 札所等 = {{ill|Izumi Saigoku 33 Kasho|ja|和泉西国三十三箇所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Saigoku 33 Kasho|qq=|1=33 pilgrimage sites of Izumi-saigoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417837}} 第11番 | 公式HP = http://sasaki3.net/old_contents/nenbutuji/index1.html | 公式HP名 = Sakai City Jōdo Sect Nenbutsu-ji }} '''Nenbutsu-ji''' (念佛寺) is a Buddhist temple of the Jōdo sect located in Nishi Ward, [[:en:Sakai|Sakai City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. == Overview == The temple’s mountain name, Chikuaoyama (乳岡山), is situated at the westernmost end of the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]] and is associated with the shrine-temple of {{ill|Ishizutsuta Shrine|ja|石津太神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizutsuta Shrine|qq=|1=Iwatsuta-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586779}}, hence once being called "The Temple of the Mountain." In ancient times, a full temple complex (伽藍) existed within the village, and the nearby area of {{ill|Kami Ishizutsu-machi|ja|上石津|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Ishizutsu-machi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was also known as Kannonji-machi. Historically, the founder’s memorial tablet recorded the inscription "En'yo Yōun Hikyuni, 6th day of June, in the 3rd year of [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]]" indicating that the temple was indeed rebuilt by Yōun-ni during the [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] period. In Showa 55 (1980), due to land development pressures, the temple was relocated to its present site. == Access == * A 10‑minute walk from the {{ill|Nankai Bus|ja|南海バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nankai Bus|qq=|1=Nankai Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408154}} stop "Kasumigaoka." * A 25‑minute walk from [[:en:Uenoshiba Station|Ueno-shiba Station]] on the [[:en:Hanwa Line|Hankawa Line]]. == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == References == * 『{{ill|Izumi Saigoku 33 Kasho Meguri Furusato no Kannon-sama|ja|和泉西国三十三所めぐり ふる里の観音さま|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Saigoku 33 Kasho Meguri Furusato no Kannon-sama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』, Izumi Saigoku-kai. {{Buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|念仏寺 (堺市)|version=89988578|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Nenbutsu-ji (Sakai)]] [[sn:E5%BF%B5%E4%BB%8F%E5%AF%BA (%E5%A0%BA%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:念佛寺]] [[Category:Temples in Sakai, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Jōdo-shū temples]] [[ja:念仏寺 (堺市)]]

In ancient times, a full temple complex (伽藍) existed within the village, and the nearby area of {{ill|Kami Ishizutsu-machi|ja|上石津|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Ishizutsu-machi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was also known as Kannonji-machi. Historically, the founder’s memorial tablet recorded the inscription "En'yo Yōun Hikyuni, 6th day of June, in the 3rd year of [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]]" indicating that the temple was indeed rebuilt by Yōun-ni during the [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] period. In Showa 55 (1980), due to land development pressures, the temple was relocated to its present site. == Access == * A 10‑minute walk from the {{ill|Nankai Bus|ja|南海バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nankai Bus|qq=|1=Nankai Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408154}} stop "Kasumigaoka." * A 25‑minute walk from [[:en:Uenoshiba Station|Ueno-shiba Station]] on the [[:en:Hanwa Line|Hankawa Line]]. == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == References == * 『{{ill|Izumi Saigoku 33 Kasho Meguri Furusato no Kannon-sama|ja|和泉西国三十三所めぐり ふる里の観音さま|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Saigoku 33 Kasho Meguri Furusato no Kannon-sama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』, Izumi Saigoku-kai. {{Buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|念仏寺 (堺市)|version=89988578|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Nenbutsu-ji (Sakai)]] [[sn:E5%BF%B5%E4%BB%8F%E5%AF%BA (%E5%A0%BA%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:念佛寺]] [[Category:Temples in Sakai, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Jōdo-shū temples]] [[ja:念仏寺 (堺市)]]

In ancient times, a full temple complex (伽藍) existed within the village, and the nearby area of {{ill|Kami Ishizutsu-machi|ja|上石津|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Ishizutsu-machi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was also known as Kannonji-machi. Historically, the founder’s memorial tablet recorded the inscription "En'yo Yōun Hikyuni, 6th day of June, in the 3rd year of [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]]" indicating that the temple was indeed rebuilt by Yōun-ni during the [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] period. In Showa 55 (1980), due to land development pressures, the temple was relocated to its present site. == Access == * A 10‑minute walk from the {{ill|Nankai Bus|ja|南海バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nankai Bus|qq=|1=Nankai Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408154}} stop "Kasumigaoka." * A 25‑minute walk from [[:en:Uenoshiba Station|Ueno-shiba Station]] on the [[:en:Hanwa Line|Hankawa Line]]. == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == References == * 『{{ill|Izumi Saigoku 33 Kasho Meguri Furusato no Kannon-sama|ja|和泉西国三十三所めぐり ふる里の観音さま|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Saigoku 33 Kasho Meguri Furusato no Kannon-sama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』, Izumi Saigoku-kai. {{Buddhism-stub}} [[Category:Translated page but missing wikidata]]{{translated page|ja|念仏寺 (堺市)|version=89988578|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:念佛寺]] [[Category:Temples in Sakai, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Jōdo-shū temples]] [[ja:念仏寺 (堺市)]] [[Category:Missing wikidata]] [[en:Nenbutsu-ji (Sakai)]]

In Showa 55 (1980), due to land development pressures, the temple was relocated to its present site. == Access == * A 10‑minute walk from the {{ill|Nankai Bus|ja|南海バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nankai Bus|qq=|1=Nankai Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408154}} stop "Kasumigaoka." * A 25‑minute walk from [[:en:Uenoshiba Station|Ueno-shiba Station]] on the [[:en:Hanwa Line|Hankawa Line]]. == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == References == * 『{{ill|Izumi Saigoku 33 Kasho Meguri Furusato no Kannon-sama|ja|和泉西国三十三所めぐり ふる里の観音さま|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Saigoku 33 Kasho Meguri Furusato no Kannon-sama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』, Izumi Saigoku-kai. {{Buddhism-stub}} [[Category:Translated page but missing wikidata]]{{translated page|ja|念仏寺 (堺市)|version=89988578|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:念佛寺]] [[Category:Temples in Sakai, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Jōdo-shū temples]] [[ja:念仏寺 (堺市)]] [[en:Nenbutsu-ji (Sakai)]] [[Category:Missing wikidata]]

In ancient times, a full temple complex (伽藍) existed within the village, and the nearby area of {{ill|Kami Ishizutsu-machi|ja|上石津|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Ishizutsu-machi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was also known as Kannonji-machi. Historically, the founder’s memorial tablet recorded the inscription "En'yo Yōun Hikyuni, 6th day of June, in the 3rd year of [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]]" indicating that the temple was indeed rebuilt by Yōun-ni during the [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] period. In Showa 55 (1980), due to land development pressures, the temple was relocated to its present site. == Access == * A 10‑minute walk from the {{ill|Nankai Bus|ja|南海バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nankai Bus|qq=|1=Nankai Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408154}} stop "Kasumigaoka." * A 25‑minute walk from [[:en:Uenoshiba Station|Ueno-shiba Station]] on the [[:en:Hanwa Line|Hankawa Line]]. == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == References == * 『{{ill|Izumi Saigoku 33 Kasho Meguri Furusato no Kannon-sama|ja|和泉西国三十三所めぐり ふる里の観音さま|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Saigoku 33 Kasho Meguri Furusato no Kannon-sama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』, Izumi Saigoku-kai. {{Buddhism-stub}} [[Category:Translated page but missing wikidata]]{{translated page|ja|念仏寺 (堺市)|version=89988578|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:念佛寺]] [[Category:Temples in Sakai, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Jōdo-shū temples]] [[ja:念仏寺 (堺市)]] [[en:Nenbutsu-ji (Sakai)]] [[Category:Missing wikidata]] [[Category:no valid interwikis]]

In ancient times, a full temple complex (伽藍) existed within the village, and the nearby area of {{ill|Kami Ishizutsu-machi|ja|上石津|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Ishizutsu-machi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was also known as Kannonji-machi. Historically, the founder’s memorial tablet recorded the inscription "En'yo Yōun Hikyuni, 6th day of June, in the 3rd year of [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]]" indicating that the temple was indeed rebuilt by Yōun-ni during the [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] period. In Showa 55 (1980), due to land development pressures, the temple was relocated to its present site. == Access == * A 10‑minute walk from the {{ill|Nankai Bus|ja|南海バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nankai Bus|1=Nankai Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408154}} stop "Kasumigaoka." * A 25‑minute walk from [[:en:Uenoshiba Station|Ueno-shiba Station]] on the [[:en:Hanwa Line|Hankawa Line]]. == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == References == * 『{{ill|Izumi Saigoku 33 Kasho Meguri Furusato no Kannon-sama|ja|和泉西国三十三所めぐり ふる里の観音さま|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Saigoku 33 Kasho Meguri Furusato no Kannon-sama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』, Izumi Saigoku-kai. {{Buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|念仏寺 (堺市)|version=89988578|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:念佛寺]] [[Category:Temples in Sakai, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Jōdo-shū temples]] [[ja:念仏寺 (堺市)]] [[en:Nenbutsu-ji (Sakai)]] {{wikidata link|Q65268013}}

Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Jōdo-shū|Jōdo Sect]] | location = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] [[:en:Sakai|Sakai City]] [[:en:Nishi-ku, Sakai|Nishi Ward (Sakai City)]] {{ill|Hamadera Ishizutsumachi Higashi|ja|浜寺石津町東|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamadera Ishizutsumachi Higashi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3-11-5 | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|21.5|N|135|27|35.5|E|display=inline,title}} | 山号 = {{ill|Chikuaoyama|ja|乳岡山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuaoyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 宗派 = [[:en:Jōdo-shū|Jōdo Sect]] | 本尊 = [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] | 開山 = {{ill|Yōun-ni|ja|養雲尼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōun-ni|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 札所等 = {{ill|Izumi Saigoku 33 Kasho|ja|和泉西国三十三箇所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Saigoku 33 Kasho|1=33 pilgrimage sites of Izumi-saigoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417837}} 第11番 | 公式HP = http://sasaki3.net/old_contents/nenbutuji/index1.html | 公式HP名 = Sakai City Jōdo Sect Nenbutsu-ji }} '''Nenbutsu-ji''' (念佛寺) is a Buddhist temple of the Jōdo sect located in Nishi Ward, [[:en:Sakai|Sakai City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. == Overview == The temple’s mountain name, Chikuaoyama (乳岡山), is situated at the westernmost end of the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]] and is associated with the shrine-temple of {{ill|Ishizutsuta Shrine|ja|石津太神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizutsuta Shrine|1=Iwatsuta Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11586779}}, hence once being called "The Temple of the Mountain." In ancient times, a full temple complex (伽藍) existed within the village, and the nearby area of {{ill|Kami Ishizutsu-machi|ja|上石津|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Ishizutsu-machi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was also known as Kannonji-machi. Historically, the founder’s memorial tablet recorded the inscription "En'yo Yōun Hikyuni, 6th day of June, in the 3rd year of [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]]" indicating that the temple was indeed rebuilt by Yōun-ni during the [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] period. In Showa 55 (1980), due to land development pressures, the temple was relocated to its present site. == Access == * A 10‑minute walk from the {{ill|Nankai Bus|ja|南海バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nankai Bus|1=Nankai Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408154}} stop "Kasumigaoka." * A 25‑minute walk from [[:en:Uenoshiba Station|Ueno-shiba Station]] on the [[:en:Hanwa Line|Hankawa Line]]. == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == References == * 『{{ill|Izumi Saigoku 33 Kasho Meguri Furusato no Kannon-sama|ja|和泉西国三十三所めぐり ふる里の観音さま|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Saigoku 33 Kasho Meguri Furusato no Kannon-sama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』, Izumi Saigoku-kai. {{Buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|念仏寺 (堺市)|version=89988578|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:念佛寺]] [[Category:Temples in Sakai, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Jōdo-shū temples]] [[ja:念仏寺 (堺市)]] [[en:Nenbutsu-ji (Sakai)]] {{wikidata link|Q65268013}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

In Showa 55 (1980), due to land development pressures, the temple was relocated to its present site. == Access == * A 10‑minute walk from the {{ill|Nankai Bus|ja|南海バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nankai Bus|1=Nankai Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408154}} stop "Kasumigaoka." * A 25‑minute walk from [[:en:Uenoshiba Station|Ueno-shiba Station]] on the [[:en:Hanwa Line|Hankawa Line]]. == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == References == * 『{{ill|Izumi Saigoku 33 Kasho Meguri Furusato no Kannon-sama|ja|和泉西国三十三所めぐり ふる里の観音さま|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Saigoku 33 Kasho Meguri Furusato no Kannon-sama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』, Izumi Saigoku-kai. {{Buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|念仏寺 (堺市)|version=89988578|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:念佛寺]] [[Category:Temples in Sakai, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Jōdo-shū temples]] [[ja:念仏寺 (堺市)]] [[en:Nenbutsu-ji (Sakai)]] {{wikidata link|Q65268013}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]]

In Showa 55 (1980), due to land development pressures, the temple was relocated to its present site. == Access == * A 10‑minute walk from the {{ill|Nankai Bus|ja|南海バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nankai Bus|1=Nankai Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408154}} stop "Kasumigaoka." * A 25‑minute walk from [[:en:Uenoshiba Station|Ueno-shiba Station]] on the [[:en:Hanwa Line|Hankawa Line]]. == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == References == * 『{{ill|Izumi Saigoku 33 Kasho Meguri Furusato no Kannon-sama|ja|和泉西国三十三所めぐり ふる里の観音さま|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Saigoku 33 Kasho Meguri Furusato no Kannon-sama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』, Izumi Saigoku-kai. {{Buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|念仏寺 (堺市)|version=89988578|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:念佛寺]] [[ja:念仏寺 (堺市)]] [[en:Nenbutsu-ji (Sakai)]] {{wikidata link|Q65268013}} [[Category:Buildings in Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Jōdo-shū temples]] [[Category:Temples in Sakai, Osaka]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

In ancient times, a full temple complex (伽藍) existed within the village, and the nearby area of {{ill|Kami Ishizutsu-machi|ja|上石津|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Ishizutsu-machi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was also known as Kannonji-machi. Historically, the founder’s memorial tablet recorded the inscription "En'yo Yōun Hikyuni, 6th day of June, in the 3rd year of {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō}}" indicating that the temple was indeed rebuilt by Yōun-ni during the {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō}} period. In Showa 55 (1980), due to land development pressures, the temple was relocated to its present site. == Access == * A 10‑minute walk from the {{ill|Nankai Bus|ja|南海バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nankai Bus|1=Nankai Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408154}} stop "Kasumigaoka." * A 25‑minute walk from {{ill|Uenoshiba Station|en|Uenoshiba Station|WD=Q856750|lt=Ueno-shiba Station}} on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|en|Hanwa Line|WD=Q1154800|lt=Hankawa Line}}. == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == References == * 『{{ill|Izumi Saigoku 33 Kasho Meguri Furusato no Kannon-sama|ja|和泉西国三十三所めぐり ふる里の観音さま|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Saigoku 33 Kasho Meguri Furusato no Kannon-sama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』, Izumi Saigoku-kai. {{Buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|念仏寺 (堺市)|version=89988578|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:念佛寺]] [[ja:念仏寺 (堺市)]] [[en:Nenbutsu-ji (Sakai)]] {{wikidata link|Q65268013}} [[Category:Buildings in Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Jōdo-shū temples]] [[Category:Temples in Sakai, Osaka]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

In Showa 55 (1980), due to land development pressures, the temple was relocated to its present site. == Access == * A 10-minute walk from the {{ill|Nankai Bus|ja|南海バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nankai Bus|1=Nankai Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408154}} stop "Kasumigaoka." * A 25-minute walk from {{ill|Uenoshiba Station|en|Uenoshiba Station|WD=Q856750|lt=Ueno-shiba Station|1=Uenoshiba Station}} on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|en|Hanwa Line|WD=Q1154800|lt=Hankawa Line|1=Hanwa Line}}. == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == References == * 『{{ill|Izumi Saigoku 33 Kasho Meguri Furusato no Kannon-sama|ja|和泉西国三十三所めぐり ふる里の観音さま|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Saigoku 33 Kasho Meguri Furusato no Kannon-sama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』, Izumi Saigoku-kai. {{Buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|念仏寺 (堺市)|version=89988578|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:念佛寺]] [[ja:念仏寺 (堺市)]] [[en:Nenbutsu-ji (Sakai)]] {{wikidata link|Q65268013}} [[Category:Buildings in Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Jōdo-shū temples]] [[Category:Temples in Sakai, Osaka]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]
